title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be misused by malicious actors to identify and exploit vulnerabilities in unpatched systems. The tool's widespread adoption could also lead to an increase in targeted attacks if its outputs are not properly secured.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool for internal use to enhance vulnerability management processes, ensuring it is implemented securely.
Educate teams on the potential risks of exposing scan results and ensure proper access controls are in place.
Monitor for any misuse of the tool by threat actors and stay updated on security advisories related to its usage.

title: A user with physical access to a smartphone can bypass authentication mechanism of Kidsview...
description:
A user with physical access to a smartphone can bypass authentication mechanism of Kidsview mobile application and grant himself full access to the device owner's account by interacting with application's push notification.

This issue was fixed in version 4.4.3

title: Deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability in QOS.CH Sarl logback logback-core ...
description:
Deserialization of untrusted data vulnerability in QOS.CH Sarl logback logback-core (HardenedObjectInputStream (logback-core) modules) allows Object Injection albeit heavily restricted.

More precisely, an attacker able to influence serialized data sent to 
SimpleSocketServer or SimpleSSLSocketServer can instantiate objects from
 classes in the java.lang and java.util packages that are not explicitly
 blocked.

Although deserialization is heavily restricted by HardenedObjectInputStream and no 
p

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9828.

title: Roundcube's HTML sanitization path for message rendering allows loopback, localhost, RFC1918,...
description: Roundcube's HTML sanitization path for message rendering allows loopback, localhost, RFC1918, link-local, and ULA URLs even when remote content loading is disabled. A remote attacker can send an HTML email that causes the victim's browser to issue requests to local or private-network services simply by opening the message preview.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in Roundcube's HTML sanitization can allow attackers to exploit email rendering to access internal or private network services, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of internal systems.
recommended_actions: Update Roundcube to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-9818.
Implement email gateway filtering to block or sanitize potentially malicious HTML content in emails.
Restrict access to internal services by ensuring proper network segmentation and access controls.

title: bzip2 contains an off‑by‑one error in the bzip2recover utility. When processing a specially...
description:
bzip2 contains an off‑by‑one error in the bzip2recover utility. When processing a specially crafted file, the application performs an out‑of‑bounds write to a global buffer, resulting in memory corruption and a crash (denial of service).

This issue was fixed in bzip2 version 1.0.9

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the bzip2recover utility could allow an attacker to cause a denial of service (DoS) by crashing the application through memory corruption. While the impact is limited to DoS and does not directly lead to code execution or data exfiltration, it could disrupt workflows that rely on bzip2recover for data recovery.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to bzip2 version 1.0.9 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit systems and applications to identify and patch any instances of outdated bzip2 versions.
Restrict the processing of untrusted or externally sourced bzip2 files to reduce the risk of exploitation.

description: A vulnerability exists in Apache Artemis whereby an application using the STOMP protocol with security credentials that grant either the consume or send permission on an address can augment the routing-type supported by that address even if said user doesn't have the createAddress permission for that particular address. A user could successfully send a message to an address or consume a message from a queue with a routing-type not supported by the corresponding address when that operation should

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40914.

title: The Mennekes Amtron series (firmware versions ≤ 5.22.3) is vulnerable to an authentication bypass...
description: The Mennekes Amtron series (firmware versions ≤ 5.22.3) is vulnerable to an authentication bypass. An unauthenticated remote attacker can change the password of the user account via a crafted POST request to the /operator/operator endpoint.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to bypass authentication and gain unauthorized access to user accounts. This could lead to unauthorized control of the device, data breaches, or disruption of services, particularly in environments relying on the Mennekes Amtron series for electric vehicle charging.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the firmware of all Mennekes Amtron devices to a version higher than 5.22.3, if available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems and restrict access to trusted IP ranges.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity targeting the /operator/operator endpoint and apply web application firewall (WAF) rules to block malicious requests.

title: Plack::Middleware::Security::Common versions before 0.13.1 for Perl did not block header...
description:
Plack::Middleware::Security::Common versions before 0.13.1 for Perl did not block header injections in request paths.

The header injection rule was ineffective at blocking header injections in the request paths unless they were double-encoded, for example,

  GET /path\r\nHTTP/1.1\r\nHost: secret.example.com

Note that it is unclear whether request paths with CRLF followed by additional headers would be blocked by reverse proxies, or how they would be processed by Plack-based servers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9658.

title: An issue in SMSGate sms-core<=2.1.13.6 allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code via the...
description: An issue in SMSGate sms-core<=2.1.13.6 allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code via the Cmpp7FDeliverRequestMessageCodec.java component

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in SMSGate sms-core<=2.1.13.6 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise. This could severely impact systems relying on SMS communication and processing.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade SMSGate sms-core to a version higher than 2.1.13.6, if available, or apply patches provided by the vendor.
Implement network-level protections, such as firewalls and intrusion detection systems, to monitor and block suspicious traffic targeting the vulnerable component.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using SMSGate sms-core to identify potential exploitation and ensure proper logging and monitoring are in place.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: i2c: ov5647: Fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: i2c: ov5647: Fix runtime PM refcount leak in s_ctrl

Three control cases (AUTOGAIN, EXPOSURE_AUTO, ANALOGUE_GAIN) directly
return without calling pm_runtime_put(), causing runtime PM reference
count leaks.

Change these cases from 'return' to 'ret = ... break' pattern to ensure
pm_runtime_put() is always called before function exit.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46239.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

vsock: fix buffer size...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

vsock: fix buffer size clamping order

In vsock_update_buffer_size(), the buffer size was being clamped to the
maximum first, and then to the minimum. If a user sets a minimum buffer
size larger than the maximum, the minimum check overrides the maximum
check, inverting the constraint.

This breaks the intended socket memory boundaries by allowing the
vsk->buffer_size to grow beyond the configured vsk->buffer_max_size.

Fix this

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46234.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: iris: Fix use-after...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: iris: Fix use-after-free in iris_release_internal_buffers()

The recent change in commit 1dabf00ee206 ("media: iris: gen1: Destroy
internal buffers after FW releases") introduced a regression where
session_release_buf() may free the buffer. The caller,
iris_release_internal_buffers(), continued to access `buffer` after the
call, leading to a potential use-after-free.

Fix this by setting BUF_ATTR_PENDING_RELEASE before c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability involves a use-after-free issue in the Linux kernel's media subsystem, specifically in the 'iris' driver. Exploitation could lead to system instability, crashes, or potential remote code execution, depending on how the vulnerability is leveraged. Systems relying on the affected driver are at risk.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-46240.
Audit systems to identify any instances of the vulnerable kernel version and prioritize patching on critical systems.
Implement runtime protections such as memory safety tools to mitigate potential exploitation until the patch is applied.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

batman-adv: stop caching...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

batman-adv: stop caching unowned originator pointers in BAT IV

BAT IV keeps the last-hop neighbor address in each neigh_node, but some
paths also cache an originator pointer derived from a temporary lookup.
That pointer is not owned by the neigh_node and may no longer refer to a
live originator entry after purge handling runs.

Stop storing the auxiliary originator pointer in the BAT IV neighbor
state. When BAT IV needs the ne

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46238.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: mpc52xx: fix use-after...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

spi: mpc52xx: fix use-after-free on registration failure

Make sure to disable and free the interrupts in case controller
registration fails to avoid a potential use-after-free and resource
leak.

This issue was flagged by Sashiko when reviewing a controller
deregistration fix.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability involves a use-after-free issue in the Linux kernel's SPI controller registration process. If exploited, it could lead to system instability, crashes, or potential privilege escalation, depending on the attack vector and system configuration.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest Linux kernel version where the vulnerability (CVE-2026-46241) has been patched.
Audit systems using the affected SPI controller to ensure proper configuration and mitigate potential exploitation.
Monitor for any unusual activity or crashes in systems running older kernel versions and prioritize patching those systems.

title: FlowIntel up to version 3.3.0 contains a server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in the...
description: FlowIntel up to version 3.3.0 contains a server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in the external reference URL probe functionality in app/case/task.py. An attacker who can submit an external reference URL can cause the application server to issue an HTTP HEAD request to an attacker-specified destination. Due to insufficient validation of the URL scheme and resolved destination address, affected versions may allow requests to loopback, link-local, private, reserved, or other restricted n

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9813.

title: Dlink DWR-X1820 router uses weak default password generated from its IMEI number and does not...
description:
Dlink DWR-X1820 router uses weak default password generated from its IMEI number and does not require users to change it. An attacker who knows how passwords are generated can easily crack the default password if they have the device IMEI number.

This issue was fixed in version 1.00B16CP.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The weak default password generated from the IMEI number makes the router vulnerable to unauthorized access, potentially exposing sensitive data, enabling network compromise, or allowing attackers to launch further attacks from the compromised network.
recommended_actions: Update the router firmware to version 1.00B16CP or later to patch the vulnerability.
Immediately change the default password to a strong, unique password that is not derived from the IMEI number.
Educate users and administrators on the importance of changing default passwords and securing network devices.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: saa7164: add ioremap...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: saa7164: add ioremap return checks and cleanups

Add checks for ioremap return values in saa7164_dev_setup(). If
ioremap for BAR0 or BAR2 fails, release the already allocated PCI
memory regions, remove the device from the global list, decrement
the device count, and return -ENODEV.

This prevents potential null pointer dereferences and ensures proper
cleanup on memory mapping failures.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46235.

In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: rc: xbox_remote: heed...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

media: rc: xbox_remote: heed DMA restrictions

The buffer for IO must not be part of the device structure
because that violates the DMA coherency rules.

title: CVE-2026-44052 - Netatalk 2.1.0 through 4.4.2 inserts LDAP simple-bind passwords into log output in cleartext, which
description: Netatalk 2.1.0 through 4.4.2 inserts LDAP simple-bind passwords into log output in cleartext, which allows an attacker with access to the log files to obtain LDAP credentials.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers with access to log files to retrieve LDAP simple-bind passwords in cleartext, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive systems and data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Netatalk to a patched version or apply available security fixes to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to log files and monitor for unauthorized access or suspicious activity.
Implement secure logging practices to ensure sensitive information, such as passwords, is not recorded in logs.

title: CVE-2026-44051 - An improper link resolution vulnerability in Netatalk 3.0.2 through 4.4.2 allows a remote authentica
description: An improper link resolution vulnerability in Netatalk 3.0.2 through 4.4.2 allows a remote authenticated attacker to read arbitrary files or overwrite arbitrary files via attacker-controlled symlink creation.

description: A heap-based buffer overflow in the CNID daemon comm_rcv() function in Netatalk 2.0.0 through 4.4.2 allows a remote authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code with escalated privileges or cause a denial of service.

description: An SQL injection vulnerability in the MySQL CNID backend in Netatalk 3.1.0 through 4.4.2 allows a remote authenticated attacker to obtain unauthorized access to data, modify data, or cause a denial of service.

title: CVE-2026-6279 - The Avada Builder (fusion-builder) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Unauthenticated Remote Code
description: The Avada Builder (fusion-builder) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution via PHP Function Injection in versions up to and including 3.15.2. This is due to the `wp_conditional_tags` case in `Fusion_Builder_Conditional_Render_Helper::get_value()` passing attacker-controlled values from a base64-decoded JSON blob directly to `call_user_func()` without any allowlist validation. This is exploitable by unauthenticated attackers through the `fusion_get_widget_markup` AJAX endpoint, which is registered for non-privileged (unauthenticated) users via `wp_ajax_nopriv_fusion_get_widget_markup`. The endpoint is protected only by a nonce (`fusion_load_nonce`), but this nonce is generated for user ID 0 and is deterministically exposed in the JavaScript output of any public-facing page containing a Post Cards (`[fusion_post_cards]`) or Table of Contents (`[fusion_table_of_contents]`) element. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected sites.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6279.

title: CVE-2026-2734 - In mlflow/mlflow versions up to 3.9.0, the `SearchModelVersions` REST API endpoint and the `mlflowSe
description: In mlflow/mlflow versions up to 3.9.0, the `SearchModelVersions` REST API endpoint and the `mlflowSearchModelVersions` GraphQL query lack proper per-model authorization checks when basic authentication is enabled. This allows any authenticated user to enumerate all model versions across all registered models, regardless of their permission level. The issue arises due to the absence of `SearchModelVersions` in the `BEFORE_REQUEST_VALIDATORS` and `AFTER_REQUEST_HANDLERS` for the REST API, and its omission from `GraphQLAuthorizationMiddleware.PROTECTED_FIELDS` for GraphQL. This vulnerability can expose sensitive information such as model names, version descriptions, source URIs, tags, and other metadata, potentially revealing proprietary or confidential details in multi-tenant environments. The issue is resolved in version 3.10.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-2734.

title: CVE-2026-1543 - The Avada (Fusion) Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via mul
description: The Avada (Fusion) Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via multiple shortcodes in all versions up to, and including, 3.15.2 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user (typically an administrator) accesses a page displaying dynamic user data (such as via the Dynamic Data feature pulling user biographical information).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1543.

title: CVE-2026-4811 - The WPB Floating Menu & Categories for WordPress – Sticky Side Menu with Icons plugin for WordPress
description: The WPB Floating Menu & Categories for WordPress – Sticky Side Menu with Icons plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'Icon CSS Class' category field in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.8 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Editor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4811.

title: CVE-2026-9152 - A missing authentication vulnerability exists in the Altium 365 SearchService. A legacy SOAP endpoin
description:
A missing authentication vulnerability exists in the Altium 365 SearchService. A legacy SOAP endpoint exposes search index operations without requiring authentication, session tokens, or any form of identity verification. An unauthenticated network attacker who can reference a target workspace's identifier can interact with that workspace's search index, crossing tenant boundaries.




Successful exploitation allows reading a workspace's indexed contents (such as component data, project and folder names, and user metadata) and injecting, modifying, or deleting search index entries. These operations affect the search index only, not the underlying vault data, but they can disclose sensitive workspace information and compromise the integrity and availability of search results. Altium 365 cloud deployments are affected; on-premise Altium Enterprise Server is not affected.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9152.

title: CVE-2026-48172 - LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin before 2.4.5 allows privilege escalation (possibly to root), as exp
description: LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin before 2.4.5 allows privilege escalation (possibly to root), as exploited in the wild in May 2026. Detection is best done via a command line of grep -rE "cpanel_jsonapi_func=redisAble" /var/cpanel/logs /usr/local/cpanel/logs/ 2>/dev/null in Bash. If you get no output, you have not been hit with exploitation of the vulnerability. If there is output, we recommend you examine the IP addresses in the list, determine if they are valid IP addresses, and if not, block them. To determine damage done, examine the system logs for use by the detected IP addresses. The issue is related to mishandling of Redis enable/disable features. The recommended minimum version is 2.4.7.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-48172.

title: CVE-2026-1881 - The Broadstreet plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versio
description: The Broadstreet plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Insecure Direct Object Reference in all versions up to, and including, 1.52.2 via the get_sponsored_meta AJAX action due to missing validation on a user controlled key. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to disclose any private post metadata.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1881.

title: CVE-2026-9149 - A flaw was found in libsolv. This heap buffer overflow vulnerability occurs when a victim processes
description: A flaw was found in libsolv. This heap buffer overflow vulnerability occurs when a victim processes a specially crafted `.solv` file containing negative size values in the `repo_add_solv` function. This leads to an undersized memory allocation and a subsequent out-of-bounds write. An attacker could exploit this to cause a denial of service (DoS).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9149.

title: CVE-2026-40165 - authentik is an open-source identity provider. Versions 2025.12.4 and prior, and versions 2026.2.0-r
description: authentik is an open-source identity provider. Versions 2025.12.4 and prior, and versions 2026.2.0-rc1 through 2026.2.2 were vulnerable to Authentication Bypass through SAML NameID XML Comment Injection. Due to how authentik extracted the NameID value from a SAML assertion, it was possible for an attacker to trick authentik into only seeing a part of the NameID value, potentially allowing an attacker to gain access to other accounts. This issue could be exploited on an authentik instance with a SAML Source, where the attacker had an account on the SAML Source and the ability to modify their NameID value (commonly username or E-mail), and XML Signing was enabled. The attacker could modify the SAML assertion given to authentik by injecting a comment within the NameID value, which effectively truncated the NameID value to the snippet before the comment, and gave the attacker access to any user account. This issue has been fixed in versions 2025.12.5 and 2026.2.3.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40165.
